text: Frequent technical issues requiring rebooting of the system (e.g., Outlook integration problems)
Features that do not work as advertised or are buggy (e.g., antivirus add-ons causing problems)
Poor customer support and unhelpful service interactions
Seamless integration with existing tools (e.g., email clients, other software)
Reliable performance without frequent crashes or bugs

Disappointment when trusted, long-used software declines in new versions

Clear, understandable product options and reliable support

Easy-to-use, intuitive interface
Existing systems have steep learning curves or are hard to use
Frustration with hidden costs, subscriptions, or 'begware'/upselling
Fear of data loss or inaccessibility (single point of failure)
Improve team efficiency and operational workflows
Reduce complexity and learning curves for adoption
Lack of visibility into operational data to diagnose and solve problems
Difficulty visualizing complex scheduling or planting timelines
Poor system performance, bugs, and technical failures causing data loss or crashes
Improve team efficiency and performance by providing clear, actionable data
Automate manual reporting tasks to save time and reduce labor
Frustration with buggy, unreliable software that corrupts data
Annoyance at forced, unnecessary upgrades with no real value
Poor customer support and difficulty getting help
Reliable, stable software that doesn't corrupt data or crash
Simple, automated reports that land in the inbox and are actually used
Feeling overwhelmed by constant system changes, updates, and unnecessary UI/UX shuffling.
Frustration with software bugs, crashes, and failure to restore when needed.
Hidden fees and unexpected costs after purchase.
Software that is reliable, stable, and performs core functions without failure.
A product that simplifies workflows and saves time, allowing focus on high-value tasks.
Software instability and frequent crashes
Ineffective customer support with long hold times and unresolved issues
Software updates breaking existing databases and workflows
Stable, predictable, and crash-free software performance
Software that is responsive and works well with specific hardware (e.g., Wacom tablets)
Frustration with outdated software lacking promised new features
Anger over intrusive app permissions and data privacy concerns
Irritation with unclear pricing and hidden costs (ads in paid apps)
Simple, intuitive tools with minimal learning curve
Clean, efficient data integration and transformation without extra tools
Frustration with confusing product naming and versioning, causing operational inefficiency and miscommunication.
Difficulty installing or reinstalling software, leading to wasted time, forced support calls, and potential security risks.
Platform or device incompatibility, blocking access to necessary business or entertainment apps.
Products that install, set up, and work seamlessly across devices without friction or complex support.
Clear, consistent product naming and documentation to reduce confusion and training overhead.
Technical setup and integration issues blocking usage
Poor authentication and login flows
Inadequate or unhelpful customer support when problems arise
A unified platform that handles both data integration and self-service analytics
Simplified analytics requiring no formulas, DAX, or complex setup

Difficulty justifying the value of dashboard work to stakeholders
Miscommunication about real needs vs. requested solutions
Automate repetitive manual reporting to save time
Gain financial clarity and control over business operations
Missing key features or channels needed for daily operations (e.g., A&E missing, screen capture feature not in Pro version)
Poor usability or unclear instructions (e.g., software installation issues, unclear feature availability, forced portrait mode)
Software discontinuation or lack of support (e.g., purchasing discontinued software with no updates)
Replace legacy systems with modern solutions that simplify workflows (e.g., replacing TiVo with unlimited DVR)
Access to professional-grade features at reasonable prices (e.g., software rivaling expensive alternatives)
Users cannot copy data from dashboards for further analysis or reporting
Dashboards are static and cannot handle ad-hoc or unexpected queries from managers
Purchased content or licenses do not transfer across different devices or platforms
AI systems that can generate accurate, verifiable analytics on-the-fly
Seamless cross-platform access to purchased content or licenses
Difficulty making changes or adjustments to data after initial entry in competitor products
Frustration with software companies making drastic, confusing UI changes that reduce usability
Inability to modify user-facing names in Power BI without breaking downstream reports
Stable, consistent software interfaces without disruptive overhauls
Easy data modification and adjustment workflows after initial entry
The pricing looks reasonable, but I need to understand what the real implementation costs are - too many vendors hide the integration fees until after you're committed.
These customer testimonials are nice, but I want to see actual uptime statistics and hear from users who've had problems - that tells me more about how they handle issues.
This interface looks overly complicated - if it takes weeks of training just to do basic tasks, that's time our team doesn't have and budget we can't justify.
Another signup form just to see basic technical specs? If they're not transparent about simple requirements, what else are they hiding about system compatibility?
Finally, a vendor that actually shows how their API works with our existing data formats - this is exactly the kind of technical detail I need to make a decision.
